text: Sleep deprivation has a moderate negative effect on endurance performance with a pooled standardized mean difference of -0.52.
Sleep deprivation causes a greater impairment in endurance exercises lasting more than 30 minutes compared to shorter exercises.
Sleep deprivation similarly impairs endurance performance in untrained, recreationally-trained, and trained people regardless of training status.
One night of partial sleep deprivation and one night of total sleep deprivation compromise endurance performance to a similar degree.
Sleep deprivation impairs endurance performance regardless of exercise mode (walking, running, cycling) and whether the exercise endpoint is known or unknown.
The effect of sleep deprivation on well-trained and professional endurance athletes remains unknown due to insufficient evidence.
The time of day at which endurance performance is assessed does not modify the negative effect of sleep deprivation.
